problem: The projection of $\begin{pmatrix} 0 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$ onto a certain vector $\mathbf{w}$ is $\begin{pmatrix} -9/10 \\ 3/10 \end{pmatrix}.$  Find the projection of $\begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}$ onto $\mathbf{w}.$

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of projection of a vector onto another vector?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain in your own words why the projection of a vector onto a scalar multiple of another vector is the same as the projection onto the original vector?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How do we find the scalar multiple of $\begin{pmatrix} -9/10 \\ 3/10 \end{pmatrix}$ that corresponds to $\mathbf{w}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why is the dot product used in finding the projection of $\begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}$ onto $\begin{pmatrix} 3 \\ -1 \end{pmatrix}$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you find the projection of $\begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}$ onto a vector that is orthogonal to $\begin{pmatrix} -9/10 \\ 3/10 \end{pmatrix}$? If so, how?
classified_questions.label: e
